```{r}
        q25_lag = quantile(lag_stable, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
        q75_lag = quantile(lag_stable, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
    )

ggplot(stability_time_summary, aes(x = time_bucket, color = threshold, fill = threshold)) +
    geom_ribbon(
        aes(ymin = q25_lag, ymax = q75_lag),
        alpha = 0.22,
        color = NA
    ) +
    geom_line(aes(y = median_lag), linewidth = 0.85) +
    geom_function(
        fun = function(x) as.numeric(max_v - as.Date(x, origin = "1970-01-01")),
        linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50", linewidth = 0.5
    ) +
    annotate(
        "text",
        x = min(stability_time_summary$time_bucket),
        y = as_days(max_v - min(stability_combined$time_value)),
        label = "Observable maximum lag", color = "gray50",
        vjust = 1, hjust = -1, size = 3
    ) +
    scale_x_date(breaks = breaks_pretty()) +
    scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, NA), breaks = breaks_pretty()) +
    scale_color_manual(values = threshold_cols) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = threshold_cols) +
    labs(
        title = "Stabilization time distribution over reference date",
        subtitle = sprintf(
            "Reference dates grouped by %s. Lines show medians; shaded bands show the 25th-75th percentile range.",
            agg_unit
        ),
        x = "Reference date",
        y = "Lag at stabilization (days)",
        color = "Threshold",
        fill = "Threshold"
    )
```

---

## Are revision patterns related to other signal features? {#signal-features}

After checking first-release behavior, maturation time, geography, and
epi season, this section asks whether other observable features of the
signal are related to revision behavior. These views are exploratory: they help
identify where revision patterns may be driven by signal magnitude, initial
lag (the lag of the first report observed for a location-reference-date pair),
or finer reference-date structure.

### Latest Report value vs. Relative Error spread

This plot compares signal magnitude with the range of Relative Error observed
over the report history for the same location and reference date.

- Point: one location-reference-date pair.
- X-axis: the latest Report value for that pair.
- Y-axis: Relative Error spread, defined as maximum Relative Error minus minimum Relative Error across reports.
- Interpretation: a larger y-value means the pair moved through a wider range of distances from its latest Report value during the revision process.

```{r}
#| label: diagnostic-magnitude
#| fig-height: 4.5
#| cache: true
relative_error_spread_df <- convergence_df %>%
    group_by(geo_value, time_value) %>%
    summarize(
        relative_error_spread = max(abs_rel_diff, na.rm = TRUE) - min(abs_rel_diff, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    inner_join(
        pair_summary %>% select(geo_value, time_value, latest_report_value),
        by = c("geo_value", "time_value")
    ) %>%
    filter(
        latest_report_value > 0,
        relative_error_spread > 0,
        is.finite(latest_report_value),
        is.finite(relative_error_spread)
    )

relative_error_spread_df %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = latest_report_value, y = relative_error_spread)) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.2, size = 1) +
    geom_smooth() +
    scale_x_log10(labels = label_comma()) +
    scale_y_log10(labels = label_percent()) +
    labs(
        title = "Latest Report value vs. Relative Error spread",
        x = "Latest Report value (log scale)",
        y = "Relative Error spread (log scale)"
    )
```

### Relative Error by initial lag

Early publication can be useful, but it may also leave more room for later
revision. This view groups location-reference-date pairs by initial lag (the lag of the first
report observed for each location-reference-date pair), then compares how
Relative Error changes across lags.

```{r}
#| label: relative-error-by-initial-lag
#| fig-height: 5
#| cache: true
initial_lag_df <- convergence_df %>%
    group_by(geo_value, time_value) %>%
    summarize(
        initial_publication_lag = min(publication_lag, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
        initial_lag_group = cut(
            initial_publication_lag,
            breaks = c(-Inf, 7, 14, 30, Inf),
            labels = c("<=7 days", "8-14 days", "15-30 days", ">30 days"),
            right = TRUE
        )
    )

initial_lag_revision_df <- convergence_df %>%
    inner_join(
        initial_lag_df %>% select(geo_value, time_value, initial_lag_group),
        by = c("geo_value", "time_value")
    ) %>%
    filter(!is.na(initial_lag_group), publication_lag <= convergence_focus_days) %>%
    group_by(initial_lag_group, publication_lag) %>%
    summarize(
        q25_rel_diff = quantile(abs_rel_diff, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
        median_rel_diff = median(abs_rel_diff, na.rm = TRUE),
        q75_rel_diff = quantile(abs_rel_diff, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
        n_reports = n(),
        .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    filter(n_reports > 5)

ggplot(initial_lag_revision_df, aes(x = publication_lag, color = initial_lag_group, fill = initial_lag_group)) +
    geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = q25_rel_diff, ymax = q75_rel_diff), alpha = 0.14, color = NA) +
    geom_line(aes(y = median_rel_diff), linewidth = 0.95) +
    scale_x_continuous(
        limits = c(0, convergence_focus_days),
        breaks = focus_day_breaks(c(0, convergence_focus_days), convergence_focus_days),
        expand = expansion(mult = 0)
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = label_percent(), breaks = breaks_pretty()) +
    labs(
        title = "Relative Error by initial lag",
        subtitle = "Groups are based on the first observed lag for each reference date.",
        x = "Lag (days)",
        y = "Relative Error",
        color = "Initial lag",
        fill = "Initial lag"
    )
```

---

## Additional revision diagnostics {#additional-diagnostics}

These diagnostics are useful for checking data support and broad
revision patterns, but they are secondary to the main interpretation sections
above.

### Overall revision diagnostics

This section collects secondary diagnostics for data support and revision
activity. These quantities are useful checks, but the main interpretation should
come from the Relative Error, Initial Bias, and stabilization results above.

```{r}
#| label: revision-analysis-print
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
quick_threshold <- 7
few_threshold <- 3
relative_error_spread_thr <- 0.1
abs_spread_thr <- 0.05 * rev_result$max_val

total_obs        <- nrow(rev_beh)
no_rev_pct       <- mean(rev_beh$n_revisions == 0, na.rm = TRUE)
quick_rev_pct    <- mean(as_days(rev_beh$max_lag) <= quick_threshold, na.rm = TRUE)
few_rev_pct      <- mean(rev_beh$n_revisions <= few_threshold, na.rm = TRUE)

revised_beh      <- rev_beh %>% filter(n_revisions > 0)
low_relative_error_spread <- mean(relative_error_spread_df$relative_error_spread < relative_error_spread_thr, na.rm = TRUE)
high_abs_spread  <- mean(revised_beh$spread > abs_spread_thr, na.rm = TRUE)

lag_stats  <- summary(as_days(rev_beh$min_lag))
conv_stats <- summary(as_days(rev_beh$lag_near_latest))
fmt_stats  <- function(s) paste(names(s), round(as.numeric(s), 1), sep = ": ", collapse = ", ")

if (n_excluded > 0) {
    cat(sprintf(
        "::: {.callout-important}\n`revision_analysis()` requires at least one report after the waiting period to assess revision behavior. %d location-reference-date pair(s) were excluded because they only have an initial report with no subsequent reports yet. The %d remaining pairs are used for all analyses below.\n:::\n\n",
        n_excluded, n_analyzed
    ))
}
cat(sprintf(
    "- **Time range**: %s to %s\n",
    rev_result$range_time_values[1], rev_result$range_time_values[2]
))
cat(sprintf("- **Min lag (days to first report)**: %s\n", fmt_stats(lag_stats)))
cat(sprintf("- **No revisions**: %s\n", label_percent()(no_rev_pct)))
cat(sprintf("- **Quick revisions (≤ %d days)**: %s\n", quick_threshold, label_percent()(quick_rev_pct)))
cat(sprintf("- **Few revisions (≤ %d)**: %s\n", few_threshold, label_percent()(few_rev_pct)))
if (nrow(revised_beh) > 0) {
    cat(sprintf(
        "- **Minor Relative Error spread (< %.1f)**: %s\n",
        relative_error_spread_thr, label_percent()(low_relative_error_spread)
    ))
    cat(sprintf(
        "- **Major absolute revisions (> %.3f)**: %s\n",
        abs_spread_thr, label_percent()(high_abs_spread)
    ))
}
cat(sprintf(
    "- **Days until within %d%% of latest Report value**: %s\n\n",
    rev_result$within_latest * 100, fmt_stats(conv_stats)
))
```

::: {.content-visible when-meta="multi_location"}

The location table summarizes average revision activity, first-report timing,
latest-report lag, absolute spread in Report values, Relative Error spread, and
the average lag until the Report value stays within the default convergence
threshold of the latest Report value.

```{r}
#| label: location-stats-compute
#| include: false
relative_error_spread_by_location <- relative_error_spread_df %>%
    group_by(geo_value) %>%
    summarize(
        rel_error_spread = mean(relative_error_spread, na.rm = TRUE),
        median_rel_error_spread = median(relative_error_spread, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = "drop"
    )

location_stats <- rev_beh %>%
    group_by(geo_value) %>%
    summarize(
        n_rev = mean(n_revisions),
        min_lag = min(min_lag),
        max_lag = max(max_lag),
        spread = mean(spread),
        median_window = median(as_days(max_lag), na.rm = TRUE),
        lag_near_latest = mean(lag_near_latest),
        .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    left_join(relative_error_spread_by_location, by = "geo_value")

# Find highest/lowest for the commentary
sorted_conv <- location_stats %>% arrange(desc(lag_near_latest))
highest_loc <- sorted_conv$geo_value[1]
lowest_loc <- sorted_conv$geo_value[nrow(sorted_conv)]
```

```{r}
#| label: location-stats-table
#| echo: false
#| eval: !expr n_locations > 1
DT::datatable(
    location_stats %>%
        mutate(geo_value = toupper(geo_value)) %>%
        select(
            geo_value, n_rev, min_lag, max_lag, spread,
            rel_error_spread, median_rel_error_spread,
            median_window, lag_near_latest
        ),
    colnames = c(
        "Location", "Avg # revisions", "Min lag", "Max lag",
        "Avg spread", "Avg Relative Error spread", "Med. Relative Error spread",
        "Med. window (d)", "Avg conv. (d)"
    ),
    caption = sprintf(
        "Revision behavior statistics by location for %s.",
        params$signal_name
    ),
    rownames = FALSE,
    options = list(
        scrollX = TRUE, scrollY = "300px", scrollCollapse = TRUE,
        paging = TRUE, pageLength = params$max_locations_table, dom = "tp"
    )
) %>%
    DT::formatRound(columns = c(2, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9), digits = 3)
```

:::

### Number of revisions per location

This figure counts revision events by location across all reference dates. It is
intended as a data-support diagnostic: locations with many revisions have deeper
report histories, while locations with few revisions may provide less stable
evidence about long-lag behavior.

```{r}
#| label: revisions-by-location-barplot
#| fig-width: 12
#| fig-height: 5
#| eval: !expr n_locations > 3
revisions_by_location_plot <- rev_by_loc %>%
    inner_join(hhs_geo_order, by = "geo_value") %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = x_pos, y = total_revisions, fill = hhs_region)) +
    geom_col(width = 0.82, alpha = 0.88) +
    scale_x_continuous(
        breaks = hhs_geo_order$x_pos,
        labels = hhs_geo_order$geo_label,
        expand = expansion(mult = c(0.01, 0.01))
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = label_comma(), breaks = breaks_pretty()) +
    labs(
        title = "Number of revisions per location",
        subtitle = "Total revision events across all reference dates",
        x = NULL,
        y = "Number of revisions",
        fill = "HHS region"
    ) +
    guides(fill = "none") +
    theme(
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5, size = 11, face = "bold"),
        legend.position = "none"
    )

revisions_by_location_plot / hhs_bracket_plot() + plot_layout(heights = c(12, 2.1))
```

### Lag distribution by location

The diagnostic below shows the distribution of lag for all states in
the selected HHS region. Here, lag is the number of days between the reference
date and the report date. The split between first reports and later reports
helps separate initial reporting timing from later revisions.

```{r}
#| label: backfill-gap-compute
#| cache: true
backfill_df <- diagnostic_report_df %>%
    group_by(geo_value, time_value) %>%
    mutate(
        backfill_gap = publication_lag,
        is_initial_release = version == min(version, na.rm = TRUE)
    ) %>%
    ungroup()
```

```{r}
#| label: backfill-gap-geography
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
#| cache: true
lag_hhs_regions <- paste("HHS", 1:10)

lag_panel_html <- map_chr(lag_hhs_regions, function(region_name) {
    region_geos <- hhs_geo_order %>%
        filter(as.character(hhs_region) == region_name) %>%
        pull(geo_value)

    lag_plot <- backfill_df %>%
        mutate(geo_value = tolower(geo_value)) %>%
        filter(geo_value %in% region_geos) %>%
        mutate(
            geo_value = factor(geo_value, levels = region_geos, labels = toupper(region_geos))
        ) %>%
        ggplot(aes(x = backfill_gap, fill = is_initial_release)) +
        geom_histogram(bins = 40, position = "identity", alpha = 0.7) +
        facet_wrap(~geo_value, ncol = 2, scales = "free_y") +
        scale_fill_manual(
            values = c("TRUE" = dis_pal[2], "FALSE" = dis_pal[1]),
            labels = c("TRUE" = "First report", "FALSE" = "Later report"),
            name = "Report type"
        ) +
        scale_x_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty()) +
        labs(
            title = sprintf("Lag distribution: %s", region_name),
            subtitle = "First-report timing vs. later revision depth",
            x = "Lag (days)",
            y = "Count"
        ) +
        theme(legend.position = "top", strip.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))

    image_uri <- plot_to_data_uri(
        lag_plot,
        width = 14,
        height = max(6, 3.4 * ceiling(length(region_geos) / 2) + 1.6)
    )

    display <- ifelse(region_name == lag_hhs_regions[1], "block", "none")
    sprintf(
        '<div class="lag-hhs-panel" data-region="%s" style="display:%s;"><img src="%s" style="width:100%%;" alt="%s lag by state"></div>',
        region_name, display, image_uri, region_name
    )
})

lag_control_html <- paste0(
    '<div class="lag-hhs-control">',
    '<button type="button" class="lag-page-step" data-direction="-1">Previous</button>',
    '<div class="lag-page-buttons">',
    paste0(
        '<button type="button" class="lag-page-button" data-region="',
        lag_hhs_regions,
        '" data-page="',
        seq_along(lag_hhs_regions),
        '">',
        seq_along(lag_hhs_regions),
        '</button>',
        collapse = ""
    ),
    '</div>',
    '<button type="button" class="lag-page-step" data-direction="1">Next</button>',
    '<span class="lag-page-status" aria-live="polite"></span>',
    '</div>'
)

lag_script_html <- paste(
    '<script>',
    '(function() {',
    '  const panels = document.querySelectorAll(".lag-hhs-panel");',
    '  const pageButtons = document.querySelectorAll(".lag-page-button");',
    '  const stepButtons = document.querySelectorAll(".lag-page-step");',
    '  const status = document.querySelector(".lag-page-status");',
    '  if (!panels.length || !pageButtons.length) return;',
    '  let currentPage = 0;',
    '  function updatePanels(pageIndex) {',
    '    currentPage = Math.max(0, Math.min(pageIndex, pageButtons.length - 1));',
    '    const region = pageButtons[currentPage].dataset.region;',
    '    panels.forEach(function(panel) {',
    '      panel.style.display = panel.dataset.region === region ? "block" : "none";',
    '    });',
    '    pageButtons.forEach(function(button, index) {',
    '      const isActive = index === currentPage;',
    '      button.classList.toggle("active", isActive);',
    '      button.setAttribute("aria-current", isActive ? "page" : "false");',
    '    });',
    '    stepButtons.forEach(function(button) {',
    '      const direction = Number(button.dataset.direction);',
    '      button.disabled = (direction < 0 && currentPage === 0) || (direction > 0 && currentPage === pageButtons.length - 1);',
    '    });',
    '    if (status) status.textContent = "Page " + (currentPage + 1) + " of " + pageButtons.length + ": " + region;',
    '  }',
    '  pageButtons.forEach(function(button, index) {',
    '    button.addEventListener("click", function() { updatePanels(index); });',
    '  });',
    '  stepButtons.forEach(function(button) {',
    '    button.addEventListener("click", function() {',
    '      updatePanels(currentPage + Number(button.dataset.direction));',
    '    });',
    '  });',
    '  updatePanels(0);',
    '})();',
    '</script>',
    sep = "\n"
)

lag_style_html <- paste(
    '<style>',
    '.lag-hhs-control {',
    '  display: flex;',
    '  align-items: center;',
    '  flex-wrap: wrap;',
    '  gap: 0.35rem;',
    '  margin: 0.75rem 0 1rem 0;',
    '  position: relative;',
    '  z-index: 10;',
    '}',
    '.lag-page-buttons {',
    '  display: flex;',
    '  flex-wrap: wrap;',
    '  gap: 0.25rem;',
    '}',
    '.lag-page-button, .lag-page-step {',
    '  border: 1px solid #d0d7de;',
    '  background: #fff;',
    '  color: #24292f;',
    '  border-radius: 4px;',
    '  padding: 0.25rem 0.55rem;',
    '  font-size: 0.9rem;',
    '  line-height: 1.2;',
    '  cursor: pointer;',
    '  pointer-events: auto;',
    '}',
    '.lag-page-button.active {',
    '  background: #0969da;',
    '  border-color: #0969da;',
    '  color: #fff;',
    '}',
    '.lag-page-step:disabled {',
    '  color: #8c959f;',
    '  background: #f6f8fa;',
    '  cursor: default;',
    '}',
    '.lag-page-status {',
    '  color: #57606a;',
    '  margin-left: 0.35rem;',
    '  font-size: 0.9rem;',
    '}',
    '</style>',
    sep = "\n"
)

cat(paste(
    lag_control_html,
    paste(lag_panel_html, collapse = "\n"),
    lag_script_html,
    lag_style_html,
    sep = "\n"
))
```

### Number of revisions over reference date

This figure summarizes how the number of revisions per location changes over
reference dates. The line shows the median across locations, and the shaded band
shows the 25th-75th percentile range.

```{r}
#| label: revision-count-plot
#| echo: false
#| fig-height: 4.5
#| cache: true
rev_count_df <- diagnostic_report_df %>%
    group_by(geo_value, time_value) %>%
    summarize(n_revisions = n_distinct(version) - 1L, .groups = "drop")

rev_count_summary <- rev_count_df %>%
    group_by(time_value) %>%
    summarize(
        q25_revisions = quantile(n_revisions, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
        median_revisions = median(n_revisions, na.rm = TRUE),
        q75_revisions = quantile(n_revisions, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
        n_locations = n_distinct(geo_value),
        .groups = "drop"
    )

ggplot(rev_count_summary, aes(x = time_value)) +
    geom_ribbon(
        aes(ymin = q25_revisions, ymax = q75_revisions, fill = "25th-75th percentile"),
        alpha = 0.22,
        color = NA
    ) +
    geom_line(aes(y = median_revisions, color = "Median"), linewidth = 0.9) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Median" = "steelblue4")) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("25th-75th percentile" = "steelblue")) +
    scale_x_date(breaks = breaks_pretty()) +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty()) +
    labs(
        title = "Number of revisions by reference date",
        subtitle = "Line shows the median across locations; shaded band shows the 25th-75th percentile range.",
        x = "Reference date",
        y = "Number of revisions per location",
        color = NULL,
        fill = NULL
    )
```

### Lag distribution by epi season

The diagnostic below shows lag by epi season. Each panel is one MMWR
week 40-39 season, allowing the seasonal timing pattern to be compared directly.

```{r}
#| label: report-lag-by-season
#| fig-height: 7
backfill_df %>%
    mutate(epi_season = factor(epi_season_label(time_value), levels = season_levels)) %>%
    filter(!is.na(epi_season)) %>%
    ggplot(aes(x = backfill_gap, fill = is_initial_release)) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 40, position = "identity", alpha = 0.7, color = "white") +
    facet_wrap(~epi_season, ncol = 2, scales = "free_y") +
        scale_fill_manual(
            values = c("TRUE" = dis_pal[2], "FALSE" = dis_pal[1]),
            labels = c("TRUE" = "First report", "FALSE" = "Later report"),
            name = "Report type"
        ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = breaks_pretty()) +
    labs(
        title = "Lag distribution by epi season",
        subtitle = "MMWR week 40-39 epi seasons; first-report timing vs. later revision depth",
        x = "Lag (days)",
        y = "Count"
    ) +
    theme(strip.text.x = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"))
```

## Summary {#summary}

```{r}
#| label: final-summary-narrative
#| echo: false
#| results: asis
# Statistical context for the narrative
final_rev_data <- stability_combined %>% filter(threshold == "0%")
med_window <- round(median(final_rev_data$lag_stable, na.rm = TRUE))
p90_window <- round(quantile(final_rev_data$lag_stable, 0.9, na.rm = TRUE))

p_no_rev <- label_percent()(no_rev_pct)
pct_under <- label_percent()(mean(bias_by_loc$median_bias < 0))

cat(sprintf(
    "For **%s** (`%s / %s`), the diagnostic reference dates covering %s to %s\nshow the following revision and backfill profile:\n\n",
    params$signal_name, params$source, params$signal,
    rev_result$range_time_values[1], rev_result$range_time_values[2]
))

cat(sprintf("- **%s** of location-reference-date pairs were never revised after their first published Report value.\n", p_no_rev))
cat(sprintf(
    "- The **median lag to persistent stability is %d days**: half of all revised ",
    med_window
))
cat(sprintf(
    "location-reference-date pairs reached their latest Report value by lag %d.\n",
    med_window
))
cat(sprintf(
    "- The 90th percentile for maturity is **%d days** (~%d weeks).\n",
    p90_window, ceiling(p90_window / 7)
))
cat(sprintf(
    "- **%s of locations** exhibit systematic under-reporting in their first published Report value.\n",
    pct_under
))

# Threshold maturation summary
stab_summary_meds <- stability_combined %>%
    group_by(threshold) %>%
    summarize(median = round(median(lag_stable, na.rm = TRUE)), .groups = "drop")

cat(sprintf("- **Median days to reach stability**: "))
cat(paste0(
    "**", stab_summary_meds$threshold, "**: ", stab_summary_meds$median, " days",
    collapse = " | "
))
cat(".\n\n")
```
